repo_prior_art.cite: pid_collider_bias_sharp angle0 rejected: Scharfstein--Manski--Anthony binary reduction; CausalSmith/doc/API.md §3.1 ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study

published_prior_art.cite: ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004Bounds: class-based missing-outcome selection-bias bounds imply the same 2x2 odds-ratio cell algebra

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 and pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 are budgeted RD trimming and ordinal principal-strata LPs, not Lee always-selected finite-OR frontiers

published_prior_art.cite: LaffersNedela2017Sensitivity and Semenova2025GeneralizedLee do Lee sensitivity/conditional monotonicity, not a bidirectional principal-strata finite selected-mixture odds-ratio frontier; HuberMellace2015 is the missing closest principal-strata sample-selection comparator

repo_prior_art.cite: none; API §3.1 only records generic Manski bounded-outcome algebra

published_prior_art.cite: Manski1990NonparametricBounds, HorowitzManski1995Censoring, SmithVanderWeele2019Selection, and HuberMellace2015 give unrestricted/outer/principal-strata benchmarks but not this finite-OR equality-case classification

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses HuberMellace2015, 'Sharp bounds on causal effects under sample selection' (OBES 2015), a direct principal-strata sample-selection sharp-bounds comparator; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the revision states a strict open-regime extension/separation against that benchmark.

soundness_flags.label: Setup / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The admissible interval A_kappa(P) may include alpha=0, making r_d(alpha)=0 and theta_A=E[Y(1)-Y(0)|A] / a_d=t_d/r_d undefined; restrict the profile to positive always-selected mass or add alpha>0 as a standing feasibility condition.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The phrase 'neither selected outcome margin is at a Frechet equality face' is not a defined condition on (r_d,m_d,t_d), and the listed equality case m_d in {0,1} contradicts the interior assumptions m_d in (0,1).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Huber and Mellace (2015, Oxford Bulletin of Economics and Statistics) to §5 and to the Conjecture 1 comparison, then state exactly which part of their principal-strata sample-selection sharp-bound geometry is strictly extended by the finite selected-mixture odds-ratio face.

Patch the domain by requiring alpha>0 and defining the profiled set as A_kappa(P) intersected with the positive principal-share region; separately state how zero-always-selected boundary laws are excluded or treated.

Provide one concrete open-cell numerical witness with p0,p1,m0,m1,Gamma,kappa where both G and H are positive and the active endpoint is an interior OR face, then compare its value to Lee, Laffers-Nedela, Semenova, and Huber-Mellace benchmarks.

To reach the flagship floor, sharpen the kernel from a binary-outcome bespoke sensitivity curve to a strict extension theorem for a named wider class, or add inference/algorithmic endpoint characterization broad enough to look like a Journal of Econometrics contribution.

recommended_next_step: Revise by adding the Huber-Mellace comparator, fixing the alpha=0 and Conjecture 2 domain language, and strengthening the open-regime separation enough to justify flagship tier.
